title: BASE Resource
description: Progressive metadata for local-first BASE resources.

title.description: Strongly recommended for shared resources and discovery, but optional so personal Markdown can stay lightweight.

review_by.description: Date by which a human should re-read this resource (aging ontology). Consumed by `base doctor`.

valid_from.description: Start of validity for reference data (rates, price lists). Consumed by `base doctor` and the context pack.

valid_until.description: End of validity for reference data. Past this date, doctor flags it and the context pack annotates «périmé depuis …».

confidential.description: Set by a HUMAN, never inferred: this resource never leaves toward a remote model provider (egress control).

license.description: Machine-readable content licence for this resource (e.g. CC-BY-4.0, Apache-2.0). Makes per-resource licensing explicit, not just repo-wide.

compatibility.description: Tiers or clients this resource targets (e.g. navigateur, cli, mcp, studio). Declares where it is meant to apply; advisory, not enforced.

source.description: Stable semantic reference to where the resource lives; concrete access belongs to connectors.

requires.description: Semantic resource dependencies used by processes and tools. This declares what a workflow needs; it does not grant access.

access.description: Expected use of the referenced resource by this workflow. Native permissions and BASE policy still decide whether the action can actually run.

use_when.description: Routing signal: a short natural-language sentence describing WHEN to use this resource. Highest-signal field for the Router; optional and advisory (progressive metadata).

routing.description: Optional structured routing hints derived with the file. `examples` are real user phrasings (lift recall); `avoid_when` are counter-examples.

policy.description: Semantic policy intent. Public BASE documents it; strict enforcement requires mediated connectors.

trace.description: Trace configuration intent; business content should be off by default.

governance.description: Documented extension point for enterprise governance; not required by the public MVP.
